frames: The agent stands upright with both feet on the ground, arms bent at the elbows.
The agent begins to lean forward, bending its knees and lifting its right foot slightly.
The agent's right leg extends backward, and its left leg bends, preparing for a stride.
The agent is in a mid-stride pose, with the left leg forward and the right leg back, arms bent.
The agent takes a step with its left leg, bending it, while the right leg is extended behind.
The agent leans sharply to its left, with its right leg extended forward and left leg bent.
The agent continues to lean, appearing to lose balance, with its body angled significantly.
The agent is almost horizontal, sliding on its left foot, indicating a fall rather than locomotion.
